title: UKâ€™s CCL Reform 2025: Adapting Taxation for Electrolytic Hydrogen and Energy Transition
content: At the Spring Statement 2025, the UK government pledged to exempt electricity used in electrolytic hydrogen production from Climate Change Levy (CCL) costs, aiming to boost low-carbon hydrogen production to decarbonize the power system, industry, and transport sectors. This consultation seeks input on the optimal legislative approach to implement this exemption effectively, minimizing unintended consequences, while also gathering feedback on a broader CCL review. With the energy landscape evolving since the CCLâ€™s introduction, the review will ensure the tax system adapts to support the governmentâ€™s clean power and net zero goals.
author: Analysts Account
